race.name: Great Nordeen
race.tagline: Thirty kilometers of net-downhill freestyle racing from Mt. Bachelor through the Deschutes National Forest — Central Oregon's largest Nordic race, honoring local ski pioneer Emil Nordeen.

vitals.location: Mt. Bachelor to Wanoga Sno Park, Bend

climate.description: February at Mt. Bachelor means abundant Cascade snow and moderate temperatures. The start at ~6,400 feet (1,950m) on the volcano's flanks ensures deep snow cover. Temperatures range from -10°C to 5°C. Pacific moisture delivers reliable snowfall, though it can be wetter and heavier than intermountain powder.
challenges: Pacific Northwest wet snow — heavier than intermountain powder
Altitude at the start (~1,950m) — moderate performance impact
Weather can shift rapidly on Mt. Bachelor's exposed flanks

course.primary: A 30km point-to-point freestyle race from Mt. Bachelor to Wanoga Sno Park on Deschutes National Forest trails. Net downhill with rolling terrain through ponderosa pine and high-desert Cascade forest. Named in honor of local ski pioneer Emil Nordeen. Also includes a 15km fat bike race.
course.grooming: MBSEF/Deschutes National Forest trail grooming — high quality
features: Point-to-point from Mt. Bachelor to Wanoga Sno Park
Net downhill through Deschutes National Forest
Named after local ski pioneer Emil Nordeen
Central Oregon's largest cross-country ski race
Includes a 15km fat bike race alongside the ski event

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: High altitude score at ~1,950m start elevation. Strong snow reliability from Pacific Cascade moisture. Scenic Deschutes National Forest and volcanic mountain backdrop. Small field and regional Pacific Northwest participation limit competitive depth.

history.summary: The Great Nordeen is Central Oregon's largest cross-country ski race, first held in 2004 and named in honor of local ski pioneer Emil Nordeen. The point-to-point course from Mt. Bachelor to Wanoga Sno Park runs through the Deschutes National Forest, leveraging Bend's exceptional Nordic skiing infrastructure. The event is organized by MBSEF (Mt. Bachelor Sports Education Foundation) and also includes a fat bike race, reflecting the multi-sport winter culture of Bend.
notable_facts: Named after local ski pioneer Emil Nordeen
Founded in 2004 — Central Oregon's largest XC ski race
Point-to-point from Mt. Bachelor to Wanoga Sno Park
Organized by MBSEF (Mt. Bachelor Sports Education Foundation)
Includes a 15km fat bike race alongside the Nordic event
Bend, Oregon is one of America's premier outdoor recreation towns
